summary: The available literature highlights issues with inconsistent dental record-keeping within military contexts but does not provide direct data on differences in dental health between new recruits. Systemic variations in healthcare access, diet, and exposures may contribute to unmeasured differentials in recruit dental health despite no explicit findings reported.
